hypothesis: Deploying multiple LLM agents with deliberately varied model temperatures in parallel to explore the same unknown system, then having a meta-agent synthesize their divergent experimental trajectories, will yield more complete coverage of the input-output space than any single agent or homogeneous ensemble, and this coverage advantage will scale super-linearly with the variance in model temperature up to a saturation point beyond which stochastic exploration becomes too incoherent to produce interpretable rules.
